title: Intelligent Folder Classification
creator: [You]
goal: Given transcribed text from a voice memo, the system correctly identifies which Apple Notes folder it belongs in using the user's existing folder structure.
successMetric: 85%+ correct folder classification. Low-confidence classifications route to a designated inbox folder rather than guessing wrong.
whyThisOrder: It depends on Epic 1 producing transcribed text. It also carries the second biggest risk — LLM classification quality is uncertain and may require prompt iteration. Better to isolate this risk in its own epic than discover it late.
roughScope: Reading the user's Apple Notes folder structure, constructing a prompt that gives the LLM the transcribed text and available folders, parsing the LLM's response into a folder selection, implementing a confidence threshold with inbox fallback, supporting prompt tuning without code changes.
outOfScope: Actually creating the note in Apple Notes — that's the next epic. This epic takes text in and produces a folder name out. Also out of scope: summarization, even though the LLM integration could support it.
definitionOfDone: Given a transcribed memo, the system reliably returns the correct folder name, and falls back to an inbox folder when uncertain.
